channel.title: Dionisio Developer
channel.description: Writing on C++, low-latency systems, performance engineering, and software craftsmanship by Eduardo Dionisio.

item.title: SDLC: The Software Development Life Cycle — A Practical Reference
item.description: In 2012, Knight Capital lost $440 million in 45 minutes because a deployment script deployed to 7 out of 8 servers. One server still ran the old code. The result: the trading algorithm bought high and sold low, 4 million times, before...
item.author: Dionisio

item.title: Cache Affinity: Why Your Data Shouldn't Travel Between Cores
item.description: Here's a brutal truth about performance that few people internalize: the bottleneck isn't the CPU. It's the path between the CPU and memory.
item.author: Dionisio

item.title: C++ in High-Frequency Trading — What Actually Matters for Low Latency
item.description: In 2020, Jane Street processed over 1 billion market data messages per day. In C++. Citadel Securities moves over $470 billion in daily volume. All in C++.
item.author: Dionisio

item.title: Real-Time Image Classification with a Vision Transformer
item.description: If you want an AI project that feels more like a product than a toy demo, this one is a strong entry point.
item.author: Dionisio

item.title: How to Build a Local RAG with PDFs Using Ollama, LangChain, Chroma, and Gradio
item.description: Everything here runs 100% locally, with no external API, no token costs, and full privacy.
item.author: Dionisio

item.title: How to Install n8n Free on Oracle Cloud and Keep It Running 24/7
item.description: If you're just getting started with automation, or you're already a dev and want an unlimited Zapier/Make running on your own infrastructure without spending a single dollar a month, this step-by-step guide is for you.
item.author: Dionisio

item.title: Will AI End Developer Jobs? For the Discouraged Beginner: DO NOT STOP STUDYING, MAN - The Market Will Create WAY More Jobs Than It Loses
item.description: Hey, dev Twitter crowd. Every day I see it on X: beginners posting “I’m going to quit programming, AI will replace everyone,” intermediates saying “tech layoffs, 2026 is going to be chaos.” I understand the discouragement. I’ve been a...
item.author: Dionisio

item.title: Clean Code Summary For Those In a Hurry
item.description: I consider Clean Code one of the greatest classics in the world of software development. Some like it, some criticize it, but everyone definitely knows some concepts that came from this book.
item.author: Dionisio

item.title: Understanding Big-O Notation - Learn Algorithm Complexity
item.description: Quick View: — Big-O notation is a way to measure how much time and memory your code can consume as the input grows. Mastering Big-O means identifying cost patterns to make informed decisions.
item.author: Dionisio

item.title: What is SRE? Site Reliability Engineering
item.description: If you're a developer and have never heard of Site Reliability Engineering, or heard of it but think it's just another buzzword, it's time to understand what lies behind this acronym that became a pillar of big tech companies.
item.author: Dionisio
